zh.lifeSummary: Lady Joan (Jane?) de Hanchford (Hankeford), Baroness Gorges,於1399年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特瑟 斯度維。她於1421年10月15日與Sir Theobald de Gorges Baron Gorges of Wraxall, Knight Banneret, Lieutenant of Normandy, KB在英國英格蘭可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1470年7月在英國英格蘭薩默塞特朗 艾希頓享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Joan (Jane?) de Hanchford (Hankeford), Baroness Gorges,于1399年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特瑟 斯度維。她于1421年10月15日与Sir Theobald de Gorges Baron Gorges of Wraxall, Knight Banneret, Lieutenant of Normandy, KB在英国英格兰可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1470年7月在英国英格兰薩默塞特朗 艾希頓享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Baroness
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nels.

Norwegian: old variant of Barsnes (and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this), a habitational name from a farmstead so named in the county of Sogn og Fjordane, possibly from a compound of the Old Norse personal name Baldrekr + nes ‘point, headland’.

nameMeanings.name: Gorges,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Claus, Erwin, Heinz, Otto, Wilhelm.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in France named with Old French gorge ‘gorge, ravine’ (see Gorge 2).
German (also Görges): derivative of Georg .

variants.name: Gorge
meanings: German (Görge): derivative of Georg .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in a deep valley, from Old French gorge ‘gorge, ravine’ (from Old French gorge ‘throat’), or a habitational name from any of various places in France named with this word.
English: variant of   Gorges   2 or   George  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: (Jane?)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Lady Joan (Jane?) de Hanchford (Hankeford), Baroness Gorges, was born in 1399, in Nether Stowey, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir Theobald de Gorges Baron Gorges of Wraxall, Knight Banneret, Lieutenant of Normandy, KB on 15 October 1421, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in July 1470, in Long Ashton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Joan (Jane?) de Hanchford (Hankeford), Baroness Gorges, est née en 1399, à Nether Stowey, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir Theobald de Gorges Baron Gorges of Wraxall, Knight Banneret, Lieutenant of Normandy, KB le 15 octobre 1421, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en juillet 1470, à Long Ashton, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan (Jane?) de Hanchford (Hankeford), Baroness Gorges,(은)는 1399년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 네터 스타우이에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1421년 10월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 Sir Theobald de Gorges Baron Gorges of Wraxall, Knight Banneret, Lieutenant of Normandy, KB(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1470년 7월 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 론그 애슈턴에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Joan (Jane?) de Hanchford (Hankeford), Baroness Gorges, родилась в 1399 г. в местности нетир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Theobald de Gorges Baron Gorges of Wraxall, Knight Banneret, Lieutenant of Normandy, KB 15 октября 1421 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла июль 1470 г. в местности лонг аштон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год.
